weather.description: Hot & sunny, perfect for outdoor opera

quickFacts: Shakespeare set Romeo & Juliet here — but the story is fictional
Summer opera season in the Arena is a bucket-list experience (€30+)
Day-trippable from Venice (1h train) or Lake Garda (30 min)
The Verona Card (€20/24h) covers most sights and buses

places.name: Arena di Verona
places.caption: Roman amphitheatre turned opera venue
places.description: This 2,000-year-old arena hosts world-class opera under the stars every summer. Even without a show, the interior is breathtaking. Grab the cheap stone seats.

places.name: Piazza delle Erbe
places.caption: Colourful market square
places.description: Verona's ancient forum is now a vibrant square with a morning market, frescoed buildings, and great people-watching. Skip the overpriced cafés — buy a gelato.

places.name: Castel San Pietro
places.caption: Best sunset viewpoint in Verona
places.description: Climb the steps (or take the funicular) above the Roman theatre for a sweeping view of terracotta rooftops and the Adige River. Bring wine for sunset.

places.name: Ponte Pietra
places.caption: Ancient Roman stone bridge
places.description: Verona's oldest bridge spans the turquoise Adige River. Rebuilt after WWII using the original stones. The view from the bridge at golden hour is perfection.

places.name: Juliet's House
places.caption: The famous (fictional) balcony
places.description: Touristy? Yes. Still fun? Absolutely. The courtyard is free but packed. The balcony photo costs a few euros. Skip the love locks and chewing gum wall.

places.name: Giardino Giusti
places.caption: Renaissance garden with cypress trees
places.description: One of Italy's finest Renaissance gardens with manicured hedges, grottos, and a belvedere viewpoint. A peaceful escape from the busy centro.

places.name: Veronetta
places.caption: Student quarter with local charm
places.description: Across the river from the touristy centre, this university neighbourhood has the best-value trattorias, street art, and a genuine local atmosphere.

places.name: Castelvecchio
places.caption: Medieval fortress & art museum
places.description: A 14th-century brick castle housing an impressive art collection. The fortified bridge over the Adige is one of Verona's most photogenic spots.

places.name: Basilica di San Zeno
places.caption: Romanesque masterpiece off the tourist trail
places.description: Verona's most beautiful church has bronze doors, a stunning rose window, and Mantegna's altarpiece. Blissfully quiet compared to the centro sights.

places.name: Lake Garda Day Trip
places.caption: Italy's largest lake, 30 min away
places.description: Catch a train to Peschiera or Desenzano for crystal-clear water, lakeside gelato, and dramatic mountain scenery. An easy half-day escape from the city.

places.name: Juliet's House (Casa di Giulietta)
places.caption: Romeo & Juliet balcony
places.description: 13th-century house with the famous balcony. Touch the bronze Juliet statue for luck, leave a love letter. Fictional but romantic.

places.name: Castel San Pietro Viewpoint
places.caption: Best city panorama
places.description: Climb the staircase from Ponte Pietra for sunset views over Verona's rooftops, the Adige bend and the Arena.

places.name: Torre dei Lamberti
places.caption: Bell tower views
places.description: 84m medieval tower in the heart of Piazza Erbe. Lift or 238 stairs. Panoramic views from the terrace at the top.

places.name: Wine Tasting (Valpolicella)
places.caption: Amarone country
places.description: Vineyards 20 min north producing Amarone and Ripasso. Visit cantinas for tastings and valley views. Book a wine tour.

foodScene: Verona's cuisine is hearty — try pastissada de caval (horse meat stew, a local specialty), risotto all'Amarone (made with the famous wine), or bigoli con sardele (thick pasta with sardines). The region produces Amarone and Valpolicella wines — do a tasting. Aperitivo with a spritz on Piazza delle Erbe is obligatory.
